parent_issues.#1640: Runtime Topology Intelligence Planning — decides observation relevance.
parent_issues.#1641: Classified Observation Object Spec — defines the observation object shape.

core_purpose: Topology Observation Emission Rules = deterministic lifecycle rules for topology-visible observations. Not: validator result, authority decision, reconciliation closure, execution permission, or proof generation.

core_invariants.state_change_ne_emission: State change != Emission. Topology-relevant transition = Emission candidate.
core_invariants.scope_invariant: No observation may be broadcast beyond its verified topology scope.
core_invariants.certainty_invariant: No observation may imply more certainty than its evidence supports.
core_invariants.determinism_invariant: Identical topology state must not produce new observation emissions unless classification changed.

emission_eligibility.core_rule: A node may emit a topology-visible observation only if the observation contains all required classification fields.
emission_candidates: observation records authority, replay, or proof transition

emission_states.description: Bypass debounce. Applies to irreversible or critical legitimacy-relevant transitions.
Defer emission until transition persists past a stability window.
emission_states.rule: Use for reversible visibility churn where premature emission would create noise.

emission_states.description: Compress multiple flapping events into one bounded visibility event.
emission_states.rule: Emit after recovery with a bounded window record. Do not emit every individual flap.

emission_states.description: Do not emit. Applies to operational repetition without legitimacy relevance.
emission_states.rule: Suppress operational repetition. Emit legitimacy-relevant transitions.

immediate_triggers.rule: These transitions bypass debounce because they represent irreversible or critical legitimacy-relevant state changes.

examples.reason: Nonce consumption is irreversible and affects replay safety.
Proof integrity failure is a critical legitimacy surface event.
Root bypass detection is a critical governance event.
Lineage conflict is a critical reconciliation-relevant transition.
Topology conflict requires immediate visibility for reconciliation.

stability_window_rules.core_rule: A transition becomes emission-eligible only if: (1) it persists past a stability window, OR (2) it affects a critical legitimacy surface, OR (3) it creates an irreversible lineage transition.

examples.scenario: validator restart with no observation gap
examples.reason: Transient restart with no legitimacy visibility impairment.

examples.scenario: critical transition detected inside an active gap window
examples.reason: Critical transitions are not deferred regardless of gap state.

suppression_criteria.core_rule: Suppress operational repetition. Emit legitimacy-relevant transitions.
suppression_criteria.suppression_test: If the observation cannot materially affect execution eligibility, authority interpretation, replay safety, proof binding, or reconciliation outcome — suppress.

flapping_aggregation.core_rule: Do not emit every individual flap. Emit one bounded visibility event after the flapping window.
flapping_aggregation.compression_rule: Do not emit every flap. Emit the bounded visibility event.
flapping_aggregation.exception: If a critical transition (EMIT_IMMEDIATELY trigger) occurs inside a flapping window, emit it immediately without waiting for window close.

deterministic_hash_model.description: For aggregated emissions, the evidence_hash must cover the full bounded window, not only the final stable state.

hash_rules.aggregate_hash_binds_window: Aggregated emission hash binds the full bounded window.
hash_rules.transition_hashes_preserve_history: Transition hashes preserve the individual flap history.
hash_rules.final_state_hash_confirms_recovery: Final state hash confirms recovery state.
hash_rules.forbidden: evidence_hash = final stable state only (loses flap history)

determinism_requirements.identical_inputs_identical_hash: Identical aggregated window inputs must produce identical evidence_hash.
determinism_requirements.changed_inputs_change_hash: Changing affected_window, flap_count, transition_sequence_hashes, final_stable_state_hash, topology_view_id, or target_surface must change evidence_hash.

emit_only_when: critical transition occurs inside the gap window
gap_emission_logic.core_rule: Gap emissions describe observed impairment. They must not assert legitimacy conclusions.

propagation_scope_classifications.description: Observation may not be broadcast beyond the originating node.
applies_to: local-only observations with no governance relevance
observations with topology_scope = LOCAL and no reconciliation trigger

propagation_scope_classifications.description: Observation may be broadcast to topology-visible circulation with full classification metadata.
applies_to: governance-relevant observations with complete classification fields
verified-global observations with SIGNED_PROOF or SIGNED_REVOCATION confidence_source

propagation_scope_classifications.description: Observation may propagate but requires reconciliation before any closure can be derived.
applies_to: observed active state with missing lineage

propagation_scope_classifications.description: Observation must not propagate. Blocked due to missing fields, conflicting state, or suppression criteria.
applies_to: raw state observations without classification wrapper

propagation_scope_classifications.description: Observation may propagate at global topology scope. Requires externally-derived verification — the observer alone may not self-classify as VERIFIED_GLOBAL.
applies_to: observations with confidence_source = SIGNED_PROOF or RECONCILIATION_RESULT
observations with verification_status = VERIFIED and proof_status = PRESENT
propagation_scope_classifications.core_rule: VERIFIED_GLOBAL cannot be asserted by observer alone. It requires confidence_source = SIGNED_PROOF or RECONCILIATION_RESULT, produced downstream of verification — not by local observation.

emission_boundary_rules_by_scenario.reason: No legitimacy relevance beyond originating node.

emission_boundary_rules_by_scenario.emission_state: EMIT_IMMEDIATELY or EMIT_AFTER_STABILITY_WINDOW depending on criticality
emission_boundary_rules_by_scenario.reason: Governance lifecycle events may affect distributed legitimacy interpretation.
Reconciliation-relevant transitions are critical and must be visible immediately.
Revocation signals must propagate immediately but must not assert verified authority state.

emission_boundary_rules_by_scenario.scenario: observed active state with missing lineage
emission_boundary_rules_by_scenario.reason: Active state without lineage cannot confirm legitimacy. Requires reconciliation.
Proof presence is a positive legitimacy signal and should propagate.
Missing proof is a gap that requires reconciliation. Must not assert invalidity.
Conflicting state must propagate immediately for reconciliation resolution.
Partition suspicion without confirmation should be deferred past stability window before propagation.

downgrade_semantics.core_rule: When conflicting higher-precedence evidence is heard, the observation classification must downgrade rather than maintain false certainty.
downgrade_triggers: conflicting lineage hash received from higher-precedence source
signed revocation heard after VERIFIED emission
proof hash mismatch detected after CONFIRM legitimacy_effect
topology conflict received after TOPOLOGY_VISIBLE classification
forbidden_on_downgrade: retain previous VERIFIED classification without re-verification
assert CONFIRM legitimacy_effect when conflicting evidence exists
block downgrade when higher-precedence revocation is received

safe_behavior.if_bridgeable_independent_evidence_exist: reconciliation may proceed using lineage-bound evidence

reconciliation_safety_rule.core_rule: Flapping topology cannot produce closure. Only stable topology or bridgeable lineage-bound evidence can support reconciliation.

#1641.role: defines the classified observation object shape

compression.#1642: decides whether the object should exist (emit or suppress)

fate_tests.expect: emission_state = EMIT_IMMEDIATELY, no stability window applied

fate_tests.given: validator restart with no visibility gap, duration < stability window

fate_tests.description: persistent gap emits after stability window

fate_tests.expect: emission_state = EMIT_AGGREGATED, single bounded visibility event with affected_window

fate_tests.description: aggregated evidence_hash changes when flap_count changes
fate_tests.given: same affected_window and transition_sequence_hashes but different flap_count

fate_tests.description: aggregated evidence_hash changes when transition_sequence_hashes change
fate_tests.given: same flap_count and affected_window but different transition_sequence_hashes

fate_tests.description: unchanged topology state suppresses duplicate emission
fate_tests.given: identical topology state observed again with no classification change

fate_tests.description: critical transition inside gap emits immediately
fate_tests.given: active TOPOLOGY_GAP window, AUTHORITY_REVOCATION observed inside gap
fate_tests.expect: AUTHORITY_REVOCATION emits immediately, does not wait for gap window close

fate_tests.description: active non-bridgeable flapping blocks reconciliation closure
fate_tests.given: active flapping window, reconciliation requested, no bridgeable independent evidence
fate_tests.expect: reconciliation result = REGISTRY_RECONCILIATION_INCOMPLETE, closure not derived

fate_tests.description: non-operability flags enforced — emission rules may not create authority
fate_tests.expect: no authority_valid, legitimacy_valid, or execution_eligible fields produced

fate_tests.description: raw state emission blocked without classification fields
fate_tests.given: observation emitted with observation_state only, no classification fields
VERIFIED observation, conflicting lineage hash received from Tier 1 source
fate_tests.expect: classification downgraded to CONFLICTED, legitimacy_effect changed to REQUIRES_RECONCILIATION

acceptance_criteria: Emission states defined: EMIT_IMMEDIATELY, EMIT_AFTER_STABILITY_WINDOW, EMIT_AGGREGATED, SUPPRESS
Immediate triggers defined: AUTHORITY_REVOCATION, REPLAY_NONCE_CONSUMED, PROOF_HASH_MISMATCH, ROOT_BYPASS_DETECTED, LINEAGE_CONFLICT, TOPOLOGY_CONFLICTED
Stability window rules defined with default model
transition_sequence_hashes preserved in aggregated hash model
final_stable_state_hash preserved in aggregated hash model
Propagation scope classifications defined: LOCAL_ONLY, TOPOLOGY_VISIBLE, RECONCILIATION_REQUIRED, PROPAGATION_BLOCKED, VERIFIED_GLOBAL
Emission boundary rules defined for all planning scenarios
Safe emission examples provided for unverified observations
Safe emission examples provided for verified observations
FATE tests defined covering all acceptance criteria scenarios
